exemplar.title: In what ways does graffiti artist Banksy utilize visual symbols, juxtaposition, color, and setting to convey the corrupt nature of power and authority in his collection of street art?

detailedExemplarData.title: In what ways does graffiti artist Banksy utilize visual symbols, juxtaposition, color, and setting to convey the corrupt nature of power and authority in his collection of street art?

subject.title: English Lang & Lit (Old)
subject.description: IB English Language & Literature combines the study of language and literature, analyzing both literary and non-literary texts across various media and contexts. Students explore how language shapes meaning, identity, and culture through critical examination of diverse text types including advertisements, speeches, novels, and digital media. The course develops analytical skills, cultural understanding, and critical thinking about communication in different contexts and purposes.

strengths: Solid contextual understanding of Banksyâs anti-authoritarian themes
Regular, generally relevant visual references to support claims
Detailed observational descriptions of key visual elements
weaknesses: Limited exploration of alternative or more nuanced interpretations
Interpretive comments rarely go beyond immediately evident meanings
Bibliography entries lack a consistent citation format
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student demonstrates satisfactory knowledge and understanding, situating each artwork within its socio-political context and linking visual details to the concept of corrupted power. References are generally relevant and back up ideas, but they remain largely descriptive and do not drive a sustained or persuasive interpretive argument. This aligns with band 3 descriptors.

strengths: Emerging analytical insights in comparing symbolic elements (e.g. fence as boundary violation)
Identification of ironic or installation aspects in select murals
weaknesses: Analysis often slips into straightforward description of imagery
Insights are brief and not developed into deeper evaluation of technique or broader commentary
Lacks discussion of audience impact and alternative readings
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the student offers some appropriate analysisâpointing out symbolism and installation choicesâthe commentary remains heavily reliant on description and does not consistently evaluate Banksyâs methods or broader implications. This performance best fits band 2.

strengths: Clear overall structure with introduction, body sections for each artwork, and conclusion
Logical paragraphing and visible line of inquiry about corrupt authority
Adequate transitions that generally maintain cohesion
weaknesses: Repetitive topic sentences and occasional digressions weaken flow
Supporting examples sometimes are listed rather than fully integrated
Conclusion redundantly summarizes without offering a final evaluative insight
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essay is well organized and mostly cohesive, with a clearly developed line of inquiry that holds throughout. Although integration of visual support is at times uneven and some transitions could be tightened, the candidateâs structuring of ideas and progression of argument exceed band 3 and meet the âwell organized and mostly cohesiveâ standard of band 4.

strengths: Language is generally clear and communicates ideas effectively
Academic register is maintained with appropriate discipline-specific terminology
Sentence constructions are mostly accurate despite some lapses
weaknesses: Noticeable lapses in grammar and occasional awkward phrasing
Informal word choices (âkidâ, âhuge roleâ) and repetitive vocabulary
Run-on sentences and inconsistent date formatting in citations
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The candidate writes in generally clear academic prose with an appropriate register and acceptable accuracy. However, there are sufficient errors and informal usages to prevent a higher band. This performance aligns closely with the descriptors for band 3.

strandSelections.reasoning: Supporting visual details are **sometimes smoothly woven into sentences** (e.g. âthe use of the primary colour red on the girlâs dress draws the eyeâ¦â), yet at other times are presented as isolated lists or captions.  Integration is therefore inconsistentâbetter than rare but not "mostly well integrated."  This situates the work in band 3.

The student demonstrates **solid familiarity with the three selected works**âidentifying dates, locations and the socio-political contexts of GCHQ surveillance, the UK stop-and-search policy and the IsraeliâPalestinian conflict.  Interpretive comments (e.g. trench-coat spies symbolising state control; dove + flak jacket implying threatened peace) show an ability to connect visual details to the guiding concept of corrupted power.  However, the commentary rarely goes beyond the most immediately evident meaning; there is little exploration of alternative readings, nuances in Banksyâs satire or wider artistic influences.  The knowledge is therefore "satisfactory" rather than consistently "good and sustained," placing it firmly in band 3.

The essay follows a clear structure (introduction, one section per artwork, conclusion) and the line of inquiryâhow Banksy exposes corrupt authorityâremains visible.  Paragraphing is logical and transitions are adequate, though repetitive topic sentences and occasional digressions weaken cohesion.  Development of the central argument is uneven, but overall organisation meets the âadequately organised with some developmentâ standard of band 3.

Visual references are **regular and generally relevant**: the essay cites the colour of the trench coats, the red dress, the cross-hairs, the satellite dish, etc. to back individual claims.  Figure numbers are supplied and aspects of context are attributed to web sources.  Nevertheless, references are sometimes broad ("brown trench coats," "bullet-proof vest") and descriptive without being used analytically to drive an argument, so support is not consistently effective.  This meets the descriptor of "mostly support the candidateâs ideas," characteristic of band 3.

The student maintains an academic register, using discipline-appropriate terms ("juxtaposition," "installation art") and avoiding colloquialisms.  Occasional informal wording (âkid,â âhuge roleâ) and repetitive sentence starters prevent the piece from reaching the consistency expected in band 4.  Hence âmostly appropriateâ band 3 is awarded.

Language is **generally clear and communicates ideas without serious impediment**.  There are noticeable lapsesâawkward phrasing (âbeige-colored trench coats to highlight the authorities spying on citizensâ), subject-verb agreement issues and some run-on sentencesâbut overall accuracy is acceptable for academic prose at this level.  Band 3 (âclear with adequate accuracyâ¦ despite some lapsesâ) best matches the performance.

The response explains what is visible in each mural and points out obvious symbolic oppositions (power vs innocence, peace vs violence), but **the commentary frequently slips into simple description of imagery**.  Analytical momentsâsuch as noting the installation aspect of "Spy Booth" or the irony of a peace dove wearing armourâare brief and not developed into deeper evaluation of Banksyâs techniques or broader social commentary.  Because analysis is present yet largely reliant on description, band 2 is the best fit.

annotations.comment: The essay opens with a rhetorical question, which can weaken the academic tone. Revise to a declarative thesis that directly states the focus.
The student demonstrates solid contextual understanding of Banksyâs anti-authoritarian work, effectively situating his practice within public discourse.
The thesis statement is lengthy and reiterates introductory ideas. Condense into one concise sentence identifying the three artworks and your central argument.
Figure 1 is placed without a clear in-text reference. Introduce the image by name and number in the preceding paragraph to improve cohesion.
The description of surveillance devices in Spy Booth is detailed, showing the studentâs observational skill.
The analysis remains at the level of description. Consider exploring alternative readings, such as irony in using a public phone booth for critique.
Typographical error: âdisquiseâ should be âdisguise.â Proofread for spelling to ensure clarity.
The student references the source banksyexplained.com inline but does not integrate its authority analytically. Use the citation to deepen evaluation of GCHQâs real-world implications.
Remove the stray mathematical markup â\overline{2}â at the foot of the page; it appears to be an OCR artifact.
The repeated phrase ânatural settingâ weakens lexical variety. Vary vocabulary to maintain reader engagement.
The initial sentence of page 3 is lengthy and dense. Break into two sentences to improve readability.
The description of sunglasses and trench coats is thorough, but the student should explain how repetition of these motifs enhances the sense of oppression.
Figure 2 lacks an explicit reference in the main text. Introduce the image by number and discuss its composition before describing details.
The juxtaposition of the red dress against a monochrome backdrop is noted, but the student could explore how this color choice guides viewer interpretation psychologically.
Run-on sentence detected in the description of the constableâs stance. Introduce a full stop and separate ideas for clarity.
The comparison between fence as security and the violation of boundaries is insightful, showing emerging analytical skill.
The phrase âOn the other handâ introduces a contrast that isnât clear. Refine transitions to signal shifts in argument more precisely.
Figure 3 caption is present but lacks a lead-in. Refer to Armoured Dove in the text before positioning the image.
The student astutely connects the West Bank wallâs protest art context to the pieceâs meaning, demonstrating good contextual interpretation.
The analysis of the doveâs bulletproof vest and target is descriptive; consider evaluating how Banksyâs composition positions the viewer as participant.
Repetition of âsymbolâ and âjuxtaposeâ indicates reliance on basic terminology. Incorporate specific art-history terms for precision.
The evocation of pathos is noted, but the argument would benefit from linking to audience impactâhow might viewersâ emotions be shaped?
The conclusion redundantly summarizes each piece. Strengthen by adding a final evaluative insight or implication for broader understanding.
Bibliography entries lack consistency in style and punctuation. Adopt a single citation format (e.g., MLA) for clarity and professionalism.
The accessed dates in two entries are formatted differently; standardize date presentation to maintain academic rigor.
Including more varied scholarly sources beyond fan sites would strengthen the essayâs credibility and depth of interpretation.
Overall language is generally clear but contains repeated informal wording such as âhuge roleâ and âkid.â Opt for more precise academic vocabulary.
